finding: loadNextPage has no post-await stale guard (unlike the debounced first-page effect): if the user edits the query or switches conversations while a page fetch is in flight, its resolution runs setMatches(prev=>[...prev,...res.matches]) appending old-query matches onto the new query's list and clobbers total/loadedPage, corrupting the results list (wrong 'X of Y', duplicate/foreign snippets, possible React key collisions).

The IntersectionObserver sets pendingAnchorRef then calls loadOlderMessages, but loadOlderMessages can early-return without prepending (isStreaming guard, loadingOlder race, empty/duplicate older page, or conversation switch); when the prepend never lands, firstMessageId is unchanged so the restore layout-effect returns at line 245 BEFORE clearing pendingAnchorRef, leaving it stuck truthy, which permanently disables the bottom auto-follow (line 198 '!pendingAnchorRef.current') so a streaming reply no longer scrolls into view until a later successful prepend clears it.

After a prepend the anchor-restore ResizeObserver keeps calling applyRestore (force scrollBy to re-pin the captured anchor) for up to 1s on ANY container size change; if the user scrolls away during that window and late async content (image/katex/shiki) resizes the container, applyRestore yanks the viewport back to the old anchor, fighting the user's scroll.

The in-conversation search builds ILIKE '%' || $2 || '%' with the raw user term un-escaped, so `%`/`_` in q act as SQL wildcards: searching a literal `50%` or a filename like `a_b` returns wrong matches, and a q of just `%` matches every message (full content_contents scan for the branch). Parameterized so not SQLi and scoped to the user's own conversation, but a real correctness/DoS-lite failure.

GET /conversations/{id}/messages response shape changed from a bare `Vec<MessageWithContent>` to a `PaginatedMessages` object under the SAME operation id `Message.getHistory` — a breaking change for any external/scripted REST consumer expecting a JSON array. In-tree consumers (ui + desktop stores, regenerated types.ts) are all updated so no internal failure, but external callers of the old array contract break.

sendMessage appends the temp user message to the current window without resetting to tail or checking hasMoreAfter; after an around=/deep-link/find jump the window is mid-conversation (hasMoreAfter=true), so a newly sent message renders directly below mid-conversation messages with the real intervening tail hidden — out-of-order display for the entire stream until reconcileTail snaps to tail on complete.

cancelEdit reloads only the newest page (loadMessages tail) to 'restore what was trimmed by startEditMessage'; under lazy-load, cancelling an edit of a scrolled-up non-tail message no longer restores the surrounding older messages and teleports the view to the bottom — pre-lazy-load loadMessages fetched the whole history so the edited message stayed in place.

On stream-complete with branchChangedDuringStream the handler now calls reconcileTail (merge) instead of loadMessages (reset). The branch actually CHANGED, so reconcileTail's merge branch (hasMoreAfter=false) unions the new branch's tail page into a window still holding old-branch prefix messages and never updates hasMoreBefore; when the edited/regenerated message was scrolled-up (non-tail) the new branch's tail page may not abut the retained prefix, leaving a gap plus a stale hasMoreBefore, where the old code did a clean full reset.

search_messages_in_conversation runs a full COUNT(*) with an unindexed content->>'text' ILIKE '%term%' EXISTS scan over the whole branch on EVERY call, and loadNextPage re-fetches total on every infinite-scroll page — so paging through results re-scans the entire branch's text per page (total never changes) and each debounced keystroke-batch triggers a fresh sequential jsonb-text scan.

Removing the optimistic conversation.messageCountChanged emit leaves this listener (and the event type in events/types.ts) as dead code with no remaining emitter; sidebar message_count now depends solely on the backend Conversation sync refetch (streaming.rs emits it origin=None so it does self-heal), but the orphaned listener/event is rot that will silently no-op.

The reverse-infinite-scroll IntersectionObserver captures `view`/root once when the effect runs; if the OverlayScrollbars viewport (osInstance) isn't initialized yet the root falls back to null (window) for a desktop inner-scroll shell, so the top sentinel can mis-trigger or fail to trigger older-page loads until the effect re-creates on the next hasMoreBefore change.

Navigating matches via Next/Prev (keyboard/Enter) updates aria-current + bg-accent on the active result but never scrolls that result into view inside the max-h-64 scrollable results list, so the active item can be off-screen in the panel.

The results-list 'Loading more results' spinner is not inside an aria-live region (only the X-of-Y count is), so screen-reader users get no announcement when a further page of matches is fetched during infinite scroll.
scrollIntoView on match activation uses behavior:'smooth' with no prefers-reduced-motion guard, so reduced-motion users still get animated scroll-jumping when stepping across matches.
The active result snippet keeps text-muted-foreground on the bg-accent highlighted row; muted-foreground over accent risks failing WCAG-AA contrast for the currently-selected match.
The results container is an unlabeled group of buttons (no role=list/listbox and no aria-label), so its purpose and item count are not conveyed to assistive tech.

prependWindow/appendWindow/mergeTailWindow are never tested with an empty incoming page ([]) — the real no-op path loadOlder/loadNewer hits when the server returns a stale has_more with zero rows; other ordering/dedup cases are genuinely asserted.
pickTopAnchor's boundary case (box.bottom exactly == viewportTop, which the strict `>` treats as not-visible) is untested, leaving the off-by-one edge of the anchor selection uncovered.

The in-flight search state renders a bare '…' in the aria-live count region rather than descriptive copy (e.g. 'Searching…'), inconsistent with the descriptive aria-labels used elsewhere and announced as 'horizontal ellipsis'/nothing by screen readers.
